objective_slice: Repair the visible credibility bug where Today's saved plan summary could disagree with the Daily plan editor after browser-local state hydration.
risk_class: Q3 because the fix was pushed and deployed to Vercel production under the existing explicit production approval record.

defect_found.summary: The Today sidebar/command center could show the persisted saved plan while the Daily plan editor still displayed suggested defaults after refresh.
defect_found.source: Visual inspection of production screenshots plus code inspection.
defect_found.root_cause: DailyPlanPanel initialized its draft with useState(plan) before browser storage hydration; later persisted plan props did not reinitialize the editor draft.

implementation: Created an activePlan value in TodayScreen so the summary and editor receive the same source plan.
Added a stable key derived from the active plan fields/statuses so DailyPlanPanel remounts when persisted plan data hydrates or changes.
Added Playwright regression assertions that saved plan input values remain in the editor after refresh.
repair.commit: 1fa968c fix: keep daily plan editor in sync

local_commands.summary: Next production build passed; routes include Today, Capture, Patterns, Experiments, Profile, Privacy
Initial concurrent run collided with a separate Next build. Rerun passed: 2 Playwright tests, including persisted plan-editor value assertions.

local_commands.command: value-shaped no-secrets scan over changed files

preview_deployment.note: Preview builds successfully but prior preview URLs are protected by Vercel login, so public proof still uses production.

production_deployment.note: This deployment updated the linked lifemax-web project.

omega_production_inspect.note: The omega project also reflected a fresh production deployment after the GitHub push.

flow: verify Today summary and Daily plan editor show the same saved plan values

truth_boundaries: This proves browser-local demo persistence and UI consistency, not backend/shared-context readiness.
WHOOP, account connection, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, database persistence, and backend writes remain unconnected.
No credentials, auth settings, DNS records, backend services, Telegram, n8n, WHOOP, Grok, or public MCP settings were edited.
The LifeMax lab repo local MCP/preflight remains red, so daily shared-context usability is still not claimed.
next_actions: Keep the goal active: the public web demo is stronger, but backend/shared-context Wave 1 readiness remains separate and unproved.
If the next priority is deployment hygiene, resolve preview protection/project split so preview URLs can be smoked publicly before production.
If the next priority is real product functionality, start the Wave 1 backend/shared-context readiness phase after local MCP is green.
